claims.raw_quote: we've built 25 new factories with the latest technologies. We've acquired another 15, and we've actually closed 60
claims.claim_text: Unilever's EUR 1 billion+ annual supply chain savings program, enabled by scale across ~400 brands and operations in 190+ countries, generates structural cost advantages that smaller FMCG players cannot replicate—manifested in EUR 130M savings from advertising production alone and laundry formulation rationalization (~80% reduction in formulations).
claims.supporting_evidence: EUR 1B+ annual supply chain savings delivery. EUR 130M savings from advertising production cost efficiency. EUR 500M Project Half overhead target (EUR 250M realized by end 2014, 1,300 more headcount reduced than planned). Formulation rationalization: reduce laundry formulations by ~80%.
claims.confidence_rationale: Scale economy mechanism is well-documented with specific savings figures. The EUR 1B+ annual savings run rate is institutionalized and accumulating competitive cost advantages. However, P&G and Nestlé operate at similar scale so the advantage over direct peers is partial. Advantage is strongest vs. regional/emerging market players.

claims.raw_quote: in the counter ranking of top global FMCG brands this year, Unilever has more brands in the top 50 than any other company in the world
claims.claim_text: Unilever's portfolio of global brand leaders (Dove, Lifebuoy, Knorr, Magnum, Ben & Jerry's, Rexona, OMO) commands persistent market share above markets in ~60% of its business and enables premium pricing—as evidenced by the ability to maintain the Dove Advanced Hair Series and REGENERATE toothpaste at GBP 10 price points even as mass market segments decline.
claims.supporting_evidence: More top-50 global FMCG brands than any other company. 60% of business building share. Most awarded advertiser at Cannes for 2nd consecutive year. Effie Awards winner for 3rd year. Ben & Jerry's +10% in 2014. Premium innovations (Magnum Infinity, Breyers Gelato, Dove Advanced Hair Series) growing ahead of mass segments.
claims.confidence_rationale: Third-party recognition and sustained market share gains provide external validation of brand advantage. The premium-vs-mass divergence in consumer behavior (premium growing while mid-tier struggles) validates pricing power for Unilever's premium brands. However, in mature categories like foods/spreads, brand equity is eroding—margarine is a structural decline.

claims.raw_quote: we've reduced the number of steps in our HR planning and performance review from 8 to 4. Sounds simple, saves an estimated 200,000 hours of work, which is equivalent to around 100 people
claims.claim_text: Unilever's 'Project Half' and continuous simplification program (overhead reduction of ~200 bps over 5 years, management and administrative headcount reduced by 4,700 in 2014) reflects embedded process power from global operational discipline that translates scale into margin improvement even in low-growth market environments.
claims.supporting_evidence: Overhead reduction ~200 bps over 5 years. 4,700 management/admin headcount reduction in 2014 (1,300 above plan). EUR 250M+ Project Half savings realized in 2014. Core operating margin +40 bps in FY2014 despite FX headwind of ~40 bps from GBP translation. Advertising production costs down 4 ppts to ~20% of total advertising.
claims.confidence_rationale: The margin improvement trajectory is real and multi-year. However, process power in FMCG is inherently imitable by competitors who make similar structural investments. The advantage is in execution consistency rather than proprietary method. Competition from P&G, which runs similar efficiency programs, limits the structural moat.

claims.raw_quote: we've further deepened our distribution reach in places like Myanmar or the islands of the Southeast Asia or the rural areas of India and Brazil
claims.claim_text: Unilever's distribution network depth in emerging markets—built over decades across 190+ countries including rural India, Brazilian interior, Southeast Asian islands, and new entrants like Myanmar and Ethiopia—constitutes an infrastructure-based scale advantage that new entrants would require many years and capital to replicate.
claims.supporting_evidence: Distribution expansion into Myanmar, Ethiopian islands, rural India, north/central Brazil. Lifebuoy in 37 countries. Indonesia sustained double-digit growth. On-shelf availability improved 9 ppts. 25 new factories built in last 5 years. Emerging markets 57% of sales (implied by 5.7% emerging markets growth vs 2.9% total).
claims.confidence_rationale: Distribution network depth is a genuine scale advantage in emerging markets where last-mile logistics are costly and complex. However, Unilever is not unique—P&G, Nestlé, and local FMCG giants also operate at this scale in many markets. The advantage is meaningful in specific underpenetrated geographies but difficult to attribute to sustained CAGR without market share data by geography.

claims.raw_quote: The Dove Advanced Hair Series is a good example. It is now well established in North America, with Dove Oxygen Moisture doing particularly well, and that is now landing in Europe.
claims.claim_text: Unilever's innovation model of 'Maxing the Mix'—systematically extending global brand platforms (Dove, Knorr, Lifebuoy) into premium variants with new functional benefits—enables margin-accretive growth by shifting consumers from mass to premium within the same brand architecture, reducing private label substitution risk.
claims.supporting_evidence: Dove Advanced Hair Series premium variants (volume, shine, fullness, 72-hr manageability). Baby Dove: natural next step for brand. Knorr iron-fortified products in SE Asia (+14% cooking ingredients). Ben & Jerry's Cores +10% globally. REGENERATE toothpaste at GBP 10 price point. Management states: 'Most [innovations] are now accretive to margins as we're able to charge more for the additional benefits provided.'
claims.confidence_rationale: The innovation-to-premium-pricing mechanism is directionally validated by management and supported by brand performance data. However, 'Maxing the Mix' is a stated strategy rather than a structural moat—P&G and L'Oreal pursue comparable strategies on equally strong brand platforms. The key differentiator is execution consistency.

claims.raw_quote: We continued to deliver the EUR 1 billion-plus savings each year across our supply chain. As we extend the low-cost business model approach to new countries, we can even do better.
claims.claim_text: Unilever's EUR 1B+ annual supply chain savings reinvestment into advertising and brand investment funds above-market growth even in low-growth environments, mechanistically supporting 2%-4% underlying sales growth guidance through the 2014-2015 period.
claims.supporting_evidence: EUR 1B+ supply chain savings per year. Core operating margin +40 bps in FY2014 despite significant FX headwinds. Advertising spend as % of sales maintained (increased in absolute terms). Share of spend vs competitors up across all 4 categories. FY2014 underlying sales growth 2.9% vs market 2.5%.
claims.confidence_rationale: The savings→reinvestment→growth loop is structurally coherent and evidenced by Unilever maintaining above-market growth in a declining market environment. However, the margin of outperformance (+40 bps ops margin) is modest and FX-adjusted core EPS growth was only 11%—indicating the mechanism operates but doesn't generate step-change returns.

claims.raw_quote: premium segments continue to do better than the mid-tier ones. But most consumers will look to make savings on many of their purchases, so they can afford to spend it on the brands and products they value most
claims.claim_text: Unilever's premium brand portfolio (Dove, Ben & Jerry's, Magnum, REGENERATE) benefits from a consumer bifurcation trend where mass segments decline but premium holds, mechanistically enabling revenue mix upgrade and margin expansion as the portfolio composition shifts toward higher-value products.
claims.supporting_evidence: Ben & Jerry's +10% in 2014. T2 tea +25% in home market first year. Talenti gelato acquisition (Jan 2015). 7 slow-growth local food brand disposals in 2014. REGENERATE at GBP 10 showing 'encouraging' trial and repeat rates. Management guiding to more Personal Care acquisitions in premium segments.
claims.confidence_rationale: The premiumization trend is real and Unilever's portfolio repositioning is evidenced by specific M&A (Talenti, T2) and organic launches. The causal connection to CAGR improvement is plausible but not yet fully realized—FY2014 was a transitional year with overall 2.9% USG, not notably premium.

claims.raw_quote: if rates were to stay as they are today through the whole of 2015... we would expect a tailwind, so that's positive of around 3% this year on both turnover and the bottom line earnings per share
claims.claim_text: Unilever's structural scale in global procurement (EUR 20B+ commodity base) and treasury capability enables the company to absorb currency devaluations that damage smaller competitors while generating FX tailwind when EM currencies stabilize—providing a cyclical CAGR buffer that is scale-dependent.
claims.supporting_evidence: EUR 20B commodity cost base, 20-25% oil-exposed. FY2014 FX headwind: -4.6% on turnover (-8.5% H1 2014). FX tailwind of ~3% expected for FY2015 if rates hold. Leverhulme rights buyback (+~1.5% EPS). Minority stake increases in India/Pakistan reducing earnings leakage.
claims.confidence_rationale: Scale in global procurement provides real advantage in commodity cycle management. However, the CAGR connection is cyclical (FX timing) rather than structural—it doesn't represent a sustained competitive advantage in the 7 Powers sense, more a temporary tailwind. Confidence kept below 0.5.

claims.raw_quote: underlying sales growth of 2.9% for the year included 1% from volume, 1.9% from price. Our emerging markets business grew 5.7%, volumes up 1.3% and pricing at 4.3% up
claims.claim_text: ULVR FY2014: Underlying sales growth +2.9% (volume +1.0%, price +1.9%); emerging markets +5.7%; developed markets -0.8%; turnover EUR 48.4B (-2.7% reported, FX impact -4.6%); core operating margin +40 bps; core EPS EUR 1.61 (+2% reported, +11% constant currency); free cash flow EUR 3.9B (adjusted for disposal taxes).
claims.supporting_evidence: Per FY2014 results presentation by CFO Raoul Huet.
claims.confidence_rationale: Reported financial figures from earnings release.

claims.raw_quote: By the end of the year, we had reduced the management and administrative headcount by 4,700, and this was 1,300 more than we had originally planned. And importantly, we're on track to exceed our target of EUR 500 million savings
claims.claim_text: ULVR FY2014 operational milestones: Project Half headcount reduction 4,700 (1,300 above plan); EUR 250M+ savings realized; overhead reduction ~200 bps over 5 years; 60 factories closed, 25 built and 15 acquired over last 5 years; on-shelf availability improved 9 ppts.
claims.confidence_rationale: Specific operational metrics reported by management.

claims.raw_quote: we expect our own underlying sales growth to be in the 2% to 4% range, as it has been in 2014. Growth in the first quarter is likely to be at the bottom end of this range
claims.claim_text: ULVR FY2015 guidance: Underlying sales growth 2%–4% (low end expected for Q1 2015 due to tough China comparators); commodity cost tailwind low-single-digits; CapEx reducing below 4% of turnover; FX tailwind ~3% if rates hold.
claims.supporting_evidence: CEO Paul Polman closing remarks, FY2014 results call.
claims.confidence_rationale: Management guidance at full year results announcement.

claims.raw_quote: Our growth remains competitive. Underlying sales growth was at 3.2% for the first 9 months, 1.4% coming from volume. Both the value growth and volume growth are 1 full percentage point ahead of our markets.
claims.claim_text: ULVR 9M 2014: Underlying sales growth 3.2% (volume +1.4%); 1 full percentage point ahead of market growth rate; 60% of business gaining market share; emerging markets growth 5.7% (first 9 months).
claims.supporting_evidence: Per Q3 2014 earnings call CFO remarks.
claims.confidence_rationale: Reported financial figures from Q3 2014 earnings. Applied -10% penalty for quarterly source per rule_12_t.
